spec_match.rationale: Probes the actual gates the API uses — `ControlledFrame in window`, secure context, crossOriginIsolated, custom element registration — which is the v140 IWA-only enforcement.

interactive.rationale: Two real buttons — probe and construct — each running the live check and displaying the verdict.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a feature probe explaining yes/no with why; the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the IWA-not-installed and constructor-missing paths but doesn't show what happens if `controlled-frame` permissions-policy is denied inside an IWA — a real failure mode.

references.rationale: Includes the correct chromestatus.com/feature/5199572022853632 link in see-also.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, .yes/.no use accent emerald and rose, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate permissions-policy denial inside an IWA
openQuestions.detail: An IWA that doesn't include `controlled-frame` in its policy still can't construct the element. The probe should display the permissions-policy result so visitors understand the two-layer gate (IWA + policy), not just the IWA layer.

openQuestions.title: Show the .swbn install ceremony
openQuestions.detail: The warning block says 'enable the flag and install the IWA' but that's three steps minimum (flag, build .swbn, install via chrome://web-app-internals). A linked checklist or screenshot tour would close the loop — currently a visitor leaves knowing 'it's gated' but not 'here's how to actually test it'.

summary: Honest live probe that gets the platform invariants right. It still misses the IWA-installed-but-policy-denied edge case and a more complete install checklist.
